headline: KPKT approved 340 strata developments but inspected only 52
context: The Housing Ministry approved 340 new strata developments in 2025 but conducted site inspections on only 52, leaving 288 developments without regulatory quality verification.

cards.big: 340 strata developments approved — only 52 inspected before residents moved in
cards.sub: 85% of new developments received zero site inspection from housing regulators

cards.big: Strata Management Act 2013 requires compliance inspection before handover
cards.sub: Ministry acknowledged inspection mandate but cited insufficient qualified inspectors

cards.big: Defective strata developments cost buyers estimated RM890 million in remediation
cards.sub: Residents pay for defects that inspection would have caught before completion

cards.big: Housing inspection unit has 18 officers for 340 new developments nationwide
cards.sub: One inspector per 19 developments makes meaningful oversight mathematically impossible

cards.big: Approving without inspecting isn't regulation — it's a rubber stamp with legal consequences for buyers
Should developers be required to fund independent third-party inspections before handover?
